label: Loading Assets
title: Loading assets
lead: Load embedded and loose sprites, audio clips, UI sprites, and text resources through CUCoreLib.
bodyHtml:
Great! We have a mod.

Hm. Looks like it doesn't do much, though. A single line of debug dialogue is hardly enough for a mod, after all.

Let's add some images that we'll be able to use now.

Embedded assets

Use embedded assets for core art, default sounds, and data that should always ship with your mod. In Visual Studio, add the file to your project and set its Build Action to Embedded Resource.

Tip: keep assets in folders such as Images, Audio, or Data so the manifest names stay predictable.

  • To embed a file, add it to your project and open its properties.
  • Set the build action to Embedded Resource.
  • They are great for item icons, building hit sounds, loop audio, default text files, and required assets.

AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite and LoadEmbeddedText search the calling assembly by suffix, so you can usually pass only the unique tail of the manifest resource name.

Sprite icon = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.sunpear.png");
string json = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedText("Data.default-loot.json");

Resource names

.NET names embedded resources using the project's default namespace plus the folder and file name. A file at Images/sunpear.png often becomes something like MyMod.Images.sunpear.png.

CUCoreLib lets you pass the shorter suffix when it is unique. If two resources end the same way, use a longer suffix so the loader knows which one you mean.

// Usually enough:
Sprite icon = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.sunpear.png");

// More explicit if needed:
Sprite icon = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("MyMod.Images.sunpear.png");

Loose files

Use loose files when the asset should be user-editable, optional, or replaceable without recompiling the mod. For paths relative to your plugin DLL, use LoadSpriteFromPluginFolder().

Both embedded and loose sprites are converted to Unity sprites with point filtering and clamp wrapping, which is friendly for pixel-art item sprites.

Sprite icon = AssetLoader.LoadSpriteFromPluginFolder(this, "Images/sunpear.png");

In this example, the file would be located at BepInEx/plugins/MyMod/Images/sunpear.png.

Shared asset cache

When more than one system needs to resolve the same loaded asset by ID later, cache it once and reuse it. CUCoreLib exposes cache helpers for shared sprites.

Sprite icon = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.sunpear.png");

AssetLoader.CacheSprite("sunpear", icon);

Sprite cachedIcon = AssetLoader.GetCachedSprite("sunpear");

Audio loading, caching, and playback patterns now have their own dedicated page so they are easier to find when you are wiring loops, hit sounds, or sound packs.

When to choose which
  • Embed required icons, shipped sounds, and bundled text or JSON files.
  • Use loose files for texture packs, config-adjacent assets, or anything players may replace.
  • See the Audio page for AudioClip-specific loading, caching, and playback guidance.
